title: Generics
learning_outcomes: Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Generics syntax parameters, bounded generic constraints (<T extends Constraint>).

content_sections.title: Generics
content_sections.content:
### Compiler Configurations, Generics & Type Variances
TypeScript adds static typing checks to JavaScript codebases.

#### Const Assertions & Widening
*   **Literal Widening:** Variables declared with `let` widen to base types (e.g. `let a = 'GET'` is type `string`).
*   **Const Assertions (`as const`):** Prevents literal widening and marks all nested properties as `readonly`, locking types to literal values.

#### Conditional Types & infer
Conditional types evaluate structural type relations:
$$T \text{ extends } U \text{ ? } X \text{ : } Y$$
*   `infer`: Declares a placeholder variable inside the conditional check to extract sub-types dynamically (e.g. extracting function return types).

#### Parameter Type Variances
*   **Covariance:** Subtyping direction matches. If `Dog extends Animal`, then `List<Dog>` is assignable to `List<Animal>`.
*   **Contravariance:** Subtyping direction reverses. Applies to function parameter arguments. A function accepting `Animal` is assignable to one accepting `Dog` because the handler is prepared to process any animal type.
*   *strictFunctionTypes:* Enforces contravariant parameter validation, preventing runtime bivariant type bypass errors.

### Asymptotic and Spatial Complexity Analysis
We summarize the computational characteristics for Generics implementations in the following reference matrix:

| Process/Operation | Worst-Case Time | Average-Case Time | Space Complexity |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Core Logic Execution | $O(N)$ | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ |
| Memory Operations | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(N)$ |

### Interactive Virtualization Configuration
To aid conceptual comprehension, the web client maps this topic to the `type-variance-checker` simulation runtime, drawing state vectors from the following callout schema.

callouts.title: Common Trap in Generics
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Generics is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.
